findings.description: sg scan is project-level (sgconfig required), sg run is per-file. sg scan --stdin is invalid. Recurred across 3 units.
findings.proposedRule: Always verify ast-grep CLI flags against official docs before writing protocols or hooks.

findings.description: which sg on Linux finds shadow-utils newgrp, not ast-grep. Must validate binary identity.
findings.proposedRule: Validate with sg --version | grep ast-grep for any tool where binary name could collide.

findings.description: JS regex \Z matches literal Z (PCRE only). Caused P1 bug in correction bridge.
findings.proposedRule: Never use \Z in JavaScript regex; use $ for end-of-string.

findings.description: CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT always set in hook context. Conditional checks create dead code.
findings.proposedRule: Design hooks assuming plugin environment; external fallbacks belong in gate skills.

findings.description: execSync with string-interpolated file paths enables shell injection. execFileSync with list-form args prevents it.
findings.proposedRule: Use list-form subprocess args in all hook handlers. No exceptions.

findings.description: Symlinked source layout requires real core/ paths for git staging.
findings.proposedRule: Always stage using real paths, not symlink paths. Check with ls -la.

security.notes: Shell injection in execSync caught during verify, fixed before ship

wiring.notes: DESIGN.md stale reference (Unit 1), missing protocol ref + hook name in tests (Unit 3) — all fixed

spec.notes: Unit 1: AC-2 spec premise incorrect (CLAUDE.md didn't reference invariant 5). Unit 4: AC-1 two pragmatic deviations (LSP detection proxy, hardcoded catch pattern). Unit 4: AC-7 no automated test for feedback-log accumulation.
